title: Stelis coripatae
intro: Stelis coripatae is a species of orchid, specifically a type of epiphytic orchid native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis coripatae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Stelis coripatae is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a tincture to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Stelis coripatae is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises over 30,000 known species of orchids. Within the Orchidaceae family, Stelis coripatae is further classified in the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Epidendreae. The genus Stelis is a small genus of orchids, and Stelis coripatae is one of the species within this genus. Stelis coripatae is native to Peru, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.
